description_html:
About Duotone Kite Foils

Duotone has spent nearly two decades perfecting the art of hydrofoil design. Their commitment to innovation and real-world testing separates duotone kite foils from the crowd. Built on the ethos that foil technology should enhance your connection to the water—not complicate it—Duotone invests heavily in wing design, mast geometry, and fuselage engineering. Every duotone kite foils model is developed with input from pro riders and everyday enthusiasts who push the limits on the water.

The brand's German heritage and Scandinavian expertise combine to create foils that feel responsive, predictable, and durable. Whether you're learning to foil or chasing new freestyle tricks, duotone kite foils are built to grow with your skill level.

Why Buy Duotone Kite Foils at Surf Store?

We stock the full duotone kite foils range because we believe in backing what we sell with genuine knowledge. Our team has logged hours on every model—we understand the nuances between a Wave and a Freestyle setup, and we'll guide you to the right choice before you commit. Stock depth matters: we carry multiple sizes and configurations, so you won't wait weeks for delivery across Europe.

After-sales support is equally important. Questions about mast stiffness, wing chord, or fuselage balance? We answer them. Need help troubleshooting setup or maintenance? We're here. Ordering from Surf Store means you're backed by water sports instructors and enthusiasts, not just order-takers.

Our Duotone Kite Foils Range

Duotone Skim Foil – Purpose-built for light-wind freestyle and flat-water progression. Lower aspect ratio, forgiving wing shape, and predictable pop make it the go-to for riders developing their bag of tricks.

Duotone Wave Foil – Engineered for dynamic wave riding with mast flex and wing geometry that excels in variable conditions. Excellent turning radius and responsive edge control in choppy water.

Duotone Freeride Foil – The all-rounder. Balanced glide, ease of use, and crossover performance make it ideal for riders who blend freestyle with light freestyle tricks and freeride sessions.

All duotone kite foils feature premium materials: high-modulus carbon construction for weight savings, refined leading-edge profiles, and tested fuselage stiffness. Sizes range from 900cm² to 1350cm², suiting riders from 50 kg to 100+ kg.

Expert Advice

Choose the Right Size: Your weight, wind conditions, and riding style determine your ideal wing area. Lighter riders and light-wind spots benefit from larger wings (1100–1350cm²). Heavier riders or strong-wind preference favour 900–1000cm² foils.

Mast Selection Matters: Duotone offers mast options in different flex profiles. Stiffer masts suit high-wind, aggressive riding; more flexible masts deliver comfort and forgiveness in variable conditions.

Start with Proven Models: If you're new to foiling, the Skim or Freeride foils are less punishing than high-performance wave models. Build confidence and technique first, then experiment with specialist setups.

Maintenance & Care: Duotone foils require simple post-session rinsing in fresh water and dry storage. We stock protective travel cases and recommend annual checks on mast connection points. Proper care extends foil life significantly.

FAQ

Q: What size duotone kite foils should I start with?
A: If you weigh 70–85 kg and ride variable wind, a 1000–1100cm² wing is a safe starting point. Lighter riders go larger; heavier riders go smaller. We can refine this during a consultation at Surf Store.

Q: Are duotone kite foils compatible with my existing bar and bridle?
A: Duotone foils work with any modern kite harness and bar. Foil mounting uses standard plate systems. We'll verify your setup during checkout to avoid surprises.

Q: How do duotone kite foils compare to competitors at this price point?
A: Duotone's engineering, warranty, and resale value are strong. We've tested them against Cabrinha, NeilPryde, and others—Duotone foils offer excellent balance of stability, glide, and trick potential. Come chat with us to feel the difference.

About Duotone Wetsuits

Duotone has been at the heart of kitesurfing innovation since the brand's inception. The brand's German heritage means precision manufacturing and obsessive attention to detail. Duotone doesn't just make wetsuits—they engineer them. Seam construction, material selection, and thermal efficiency are all refined through real-world testing. When you wear a Duotone kitesurfing wetsuit, you're wearing the result of that commitment to excellence.

Why Buy Duotone Wetsuits at Surf Store?

At Surf Store, we stock Duotone wetsuits because they align with our own philosophy: performance without compromise. Our team has years of experience in kitesurfing, windsurfing, and wing foiling. We know how Duotone wetsuits fit across different body types, how they perform in Baltic winters and Mediterranean summers, and how they hold up season after season.

We carry current season stock across multiple thicknesses and styles. Our after-sales support means if you need advice on care, repair, or replacement, you're speaking to people who understand your sport. We ship across Europe, with competitive EUR pricing and fast delivery to Slovenia and beyond.

Our Duotone Wetsuits Range

Duotone kitesurfing wetsuits come in several key categories:

  • 3/2mm Wetsuits: Our bestsellers for spring and autumn sessions. The Duotone 3/2 offers excellent flexibility for dynamic movement, with reinforced chest and shoulder panels for comfort under harness pressure.
  • 5/3mm Wetsuits: Built for cold-water riding. These Duotone kitesurfing wetsuits provide serious thermal protection whilst maintaining the mobility you need for tricks and wave riding. Sealed seams and premium neoprene keep you warm in North Sea and Atlantic conditions.
  • 6/5mm & 7/5mm Wetsuits: Full winter armour for extreme conditions. Duotone's thickest options use gradient neoprene technology—softer where you need mobility, denser where you need insulation.
  • Hooded Wetsuits: Duotone hooded options are game-changers in winter. The integrated hood eliminates heat loss and water flush at the neck without restricting shoulder movement.

Expert Advice: Choosing Your Duotone Wetsuit

Water Temperature & Thickness: In European waters, a 5/3mm Duotone kitesurfing wetsuit handles most of the year. Summer sessions might drop to 3/2mm; winter demands 6/5mm or thicker. Check our water temperature guide—location and season matter.

Fit is Everything: A Duotone wetsuit should feel snug without restricting breathing or shoulder rotation. The suit should hug your torso but allow your arms to move freely. Our sizing charts are accurate, but if you're between sizes, contact us—we can recommend based on your body shape and riding style.

Seam Sealing: Look for Duotone wetsuits with sealed or taped seams if you're in cold water. Water flushing through poor seams kills thermal efficiency fast.

Maintenance: Rinse your Duotone wetsuit in fresh water after every session. Hang dry away from direct heat. Proper care extends life by years and maintains the neoprene's flexibility and warmth properties.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How long do Duotone wetsuits last?
A: With proper care—fresh-water rinse after each session and air drying—a Duotone kitesurfing wetsuit typically lasts 2–4 seasons. The sealed seams and premium neoprene resist degradation better than budget alternatives. High-use riders might replace annually; casual riders stretch suits to 4+ years.

Q: Can I use a Duotone kitesurfing wetsuit for other water sports?
A: Absolutely. Many riders use the same Duotone wetsuits for windsurfing, wing foiling, and SUP. The cut is optimised for upper-body freedom, making them versatile across board sports. We have customers who rotate the same suit across three disciplines.

Q: What's the difference between a 5/3mm and 6/5mm Duotone wetsuit?
A: The first number is torso thickness; the second is limb thickness. A 5/3mm gives more arm and leg mobility—better for dynamic movement. A 6/5mm trades mobility for warmth. For winter North European conditions, 6/5mm is the sensible choice; for spring/autumn, 5/3mm is more comfortable during long sessions.

Find your perfect Duotone kitesurfing wetsuit at Surf Store. Browse our current stock, contact us with fit questions, or visit our website to compare models and place an order. We're here to help you stay warm, comfortable, and in the water longer.

The brand's German precision meets decades of kiteboarding innovation, resulting in products that outlast competitors and keep you comfortable through conditions from glassy summer light winds to heaving winter swells.

Why Buy Duotone Pads & Straps at Surf Store?

We stock Duotone pads and straps year-round because our team rides them daily. We know which models suit which conditions, which durability specs matter, and which upgrades actually improve your session versus marketing noise. Our warehouse in Slovenia means rapid delivery across Europe, and our returns policy ensures you get the right fit first time.

When you buy from us, you're getting expert after-sales support too. Need advice on replacing worn straps? Questions about pad density for your size and weight? Our staff have logged thousands of kilometres on Duotone equipment and will guide you to the best choice for your style and budget.

Our Duotone Pads & Straps Range

Duotone's pads and straps lineup covers every use case. The Duotone foot pads come in multiple foam densities—soft for comfort in light winds, firmer for all-day sessions and freestyle where control matters most. Their mid-range pads balance grip and feel, letting you sense kite movements instantly.

Strap options include the popular Duotone Velcro straps for quick on-water adjustments and the ultra-secure woven designs for explosive tricks and wave riding. We carry the full range of widths and thicknesses, from premium carbon-reinforced models to durable workhorse options for beginners building kit.

Specialist products like their heel cups and arch support pads reduce fatigue on long sessions and help prevent injury—particularly valuable if you're progressing to wave or freestyle disciplines where feet absorb constant impact.

Expert Advice

Choose pad density based on your session length and wind strength. Light winds and long sessions? Softer Duotone pads reduce foot pressure and cramp. Strong winds and short bursts? Firmer pads improve board control and responsiveness. If you're between sizes, go firmer rather than softer—you can always add socks, but loose pads cost you bar feedback.

Strap width matters more than riders expect. Thinner straps suit narrow feet and light riders; wider Duotone straps distribute pressure across larger foot platforms and suit heavier riders doing powered tricks. Velcro versions let you fine-tune tension without exiting the water.

Replace pads and straps every 18–24 months, depending on session frequency and care. Duotone's construction holds up longer than budget brands, but delamination or flattened foam means reduced feel and safety. We stock replacement pads for most Duotone boards so you can refresh your setup without buying a new deck.

FAQ

Q: Can I use Duotone pads and straps on non-Duotone boards?
A: Yes, if your board has compatible mounting holes. Most modern kiteboards use standard spacing, but measure first. Our team can advise on compatibility—contact Surf Store before purchasing.

Q: How often should I replace Duotone kite pads?
A: Every 18–24 months for frequent riders (3+ sessions weekly). Duotone pads outlast most competitors, but compressed foam loses cushioning and grip. Signs include visible flattening or water pooling on the surface.

Q: Are Duotone straps adjustable?
A: Yes. Their Velcro versions offer infinite micro-adjustment, while woven straps use fixed loops. Velcro suits daily riders switching between conditions; woven suits locked-in, high-performance sessions. We stock both styles.

About Duotone Kite Bags

Duotone has spent decades perfecting the art of kiteboarding equipment, and their kiteboarding bags reflect that same commitment to quality and performance. What sets Duotone apart is their relentless focus on protection without unnecessary bulk. Their gear bags feature reinforced construction, thoughtful compartmentalisation, and materials that resist UV damage and salt degradation. Whether you're a frequent traveller hitting different spots across Europe or a local pushing sessions every weekend, Duotone bags keep your investment safe and your routine organised.

Why Buy Duotone Kite Bags at Surf Store?

We stock Duotone kiteboarding bags because we've tested them ourselves. Our team rides Duotone equipment regularly, and we know which models hold up best across the varied European conditions—from Atlantic swells to Mediterranean heat. We carry depth in the range, from compact travel solutions to full quiver bags.

When you buy from Surf Store, you're not just getting a product; you're getting advice from people who've packed these bags into vans, dragged them across beaches, and trusted them with expensive kites. We offer detailed guidance on sizing, material choice, and care, plus genuine after-sales support. If something isn't right, we sort it quickly.

Our Duotone Kite Bags Range

Duotone's kite bags line splits into clear categories to match your needs:

  • Single Kite Bags: Lightweight, compact protection for one kite. Ideal for local sessions where you're not packing much else. These bags compress well and fit easily into car boots.
  • Double & Triple Kite Bags: The workhorse options for riders with multiple sizes. Duotone's multi-kite gear bags use padded dividers and robust zips to keep each kite separate and protected during transport.
  • Travel Kite Bags: Heavy-duty options designed for airport and vehicle travel. These include reinforced bases, carry handles that won't fail, and weather-sealed compartments for boarding passes and accessories.
  • Bar & Accessory Bags: Dedicated storage for bars, lines, and pump equipment. Duotone kiteboarding bags in this category feature tool pockets and easy-access organisation.

Expert Advice

When choosing a Duotone kite bag, think about your real usage. If you travel monthly, invest in a dedicated travel bag with reinforced edges—it'll outlast three cheap alternatives. For everyday sessions, a single or double bag sized to your quiver keeps everything compact and ready to grab.

Pay attention to material: Duotone uses ripstop nylon and padded jacquard in most models, which resists tears and cushions impact. Check the zip quality too—Duotone favours YKK zips, which survive salt and sand better than budget hardware.

Store bags in a dry place out of direct sun when not in use. Salt and UV can degrade even premium materials over time. A quick rinse with fresh water after each beach session extends the life of any gear bag dramatically.

FAQ

Q: Are Duotone kite bags suitable for airline travel?
A: Yes, if you choose the dedicated travel models. These are built to withstand baggage handling and include reinforced bases. Always check your airline's weight and dimension limits first, and consider a protective hard case for expensive kites on frequent flights.

Q: What's the difference between a padded and non-padded Duotone kite bag?
A: Padded kiteboarding bags offer better impact protection during transport and stacking, making them ideal if you're travelling or storing kites near hard surfaces. Non-padded versions are lighter and cheaper, suitable for local sessions where the bag sits alone in your car or home.

Q: How do I clean and maintain a Duotone gear bag?
A: Rinse with fresh water after beach use, especially if you've been in saltwater. Air dry completely before storing—moisture trapped inside can encourage mildew. For stubborn sand, use a soft brush on the exterior. Avoid direct heat sources, which can warp the material.

About Duotone Windsurf Boards

Duotone has earned a reputation as one of Europe's most thoughtful windsurf board manufacturers. Whether you're chasing light-wind glide, freestyle tricks, or wave riding, Duotone windsurf boards are engineered to deliver real results in European waters.

What sets them apart is their obsession with construction quality. Duotone uses hand-laminated cores and epoxy resin systems that resist dings and delamination, meaning your board stays responsive for years. Their design team listens to rider feedback—every model undergoes countless hours of testing before it reaches your hands.

Why Buy Duotone Windsurf Boards at Surf Store?

We stock Duotone windsurf boards because we use them ourselves. Our team rides in Baltic winds, Atlantic swells, and Alpine lakes. That hands-on experience means we can tell you exactly which board suits your local conditions, your style, and your budget.

We carry deep inventory across their full range, from entry-level learner boards to high-performance freestyle and wave models. Every board is checked before dispatch and arrives protected. We also offer expert pre-delivery setup—mast and boom alignment, fin configuration advice, and ding repair guidance—because buying a board is the start of a relationship, not the end of it.

Our Duotone Windsurf Boards Range

Freewave & Wave Series
Models like the Duotone Jaime and Code boards are built for vertical manoeuvres and tight turning in choppy waters. Smaller volumes (50–75 litres), reinforced rails, and full rocker make them agile in waves and responsive in gusty conditions. Ideal if you're committed to the freestyle or wave scene.

All-Round & Freestyle
The Duotone Spike and Warp series offer the sweet spot for most riders. Forgiving enough for progression, lively enough for tricks. Mid-range volumes (80–110 litres) suit intermediate to advanced riders tackling light wind and bumpy days. Perfect for summer shredding across Europe.

Wave & Slalom
High-performance race and wave-focused boards designed for riders wanting speed and manoeuvrability combined. Duotone's stiffer construction and refined foil profiles deliver early planing and drive, even in marginal conditions.

Expert Advice

Choosing the right Duotone windsurf board depends on three factors: your weight, your wind conditions, and your riding style.

  • Volume: Use body weight as your guide—typically 1.5× your weight in litres for light wind, 1.0× for medium wind, 0.9× for strong wind. Duotone specs are accurate; trust them.
  • Width & Length: Wider boards are more forgiving and stable; longer boards carry volume further forward, helping early planing. Narrower boards suit experienced riders and marginal-wind specialists.
  • Rocker Profile: Flat rockers are fast and provide early planing; curved rockers suit wave and freestyle because they turn tighter and handle chop.
  • Construction: Duotone offers both epoxy and polyester options. Epoxy is more durable and responsive; polyester is more affordable and repairable. Both are excellent.

Visit our Duotone windsurf boards selection and use the filter tools to narrow by volume, length, and style. Unsure? Message us with your weight, local wind, and ambitions—we'll recommend the perfect model.

FAQ

Q: How do I choose the right size Duotone windsurf board?
A: Start with volume tables (1.5× your body weight in litres for light wind). Factor in your skill level—beginners benefit from extra volume for stability, while experts can go smaller for manoeuvrability. Our staff can help; contact us with your weight and wind conditions.

Q: Are Duotone windsurf boards suitable for beginners?
A: Yes. Models like the Spike offer forgiving platforms with stable platforms and predictable handling. They're also durable enough to handle learning crashes without costly repairs.

Q: What warranty does Duotone offer?
A: Duotone windsurf boards come with a manufacturer's warranty covering manufacturing defects. We always help with claims and arrange warranty repairs or replacements quickly across Europe.

About Duotone Windsurf Harnesses

Duotone has spent over two decades perfecting windsurf harnesses. The brand's commitment to sustainability has also evolved; newer models feature recycled materials without sacrificing durability.

Why Buy Duotone Windsurf Harnesses at Surf Store?

At Surf Store, we stock Duotone windsurf harnesses because our team uses them. Our staff aren't salespeople reading spec sheets—we're experienced water sports instructors who've tested these harnesses in Alpine lakes, Atlantic swells, and Mediterranean flats. When you buy from us, you're buying from people who understand the difference between a harness that fits well and one that actually works.

We carry multiple sizes and styles across our range, so you'll find the right fit without compromise. Our warehouse in Slovenia means fast shipping across Europe, and our returns policy is straightforward: if your Duotone windsurf harnesses don't perform as expected within 14 days, we'll sort it. We also offer expert guidance—email or call us before you buy, and we'll talk through your needs, your current setup, and whether Duotone is the right choice for you.

Our Duotone Windsurf Harnesses Range

We stock both waist and seat harnesses from Duotone's current lineup:

  • Waist Harnesses: The Duotone Sentient and Duotone Phenom series offer lightweight support with minimal bulk. The Sentient is our recommendation for riders seeking maximum manoeuvrability and minimal weight; the Phenom adds extra lumbar support for those logging serious session hours. Both feature Duotone's signature quick-release buckles.
  • Seat Harnesses: The Duotone Peak and Duotone Explorer models provide the security and support of a full seat. The Peak targets performance-oriented sailors; the Explorer prioritises comfort for beginners and all-day cruising. Duotone windsurf harnesses in this category use reinforced spreading bars to reduce pressure points.
  • Accessories: We also stock Duotone harness pads, replacement spreader bars, and care products to extend the life of your harness.

Expert Advice

Choosing the right Duotone windsurf harnesses depends on three factors: your sailing style, your body shape, and your budget.

Fit comes first. A poorly fitted harness will never feel right, no matter how good the brand. Duotone windsurf harnesses come in XS to XXL; if you're between sizes, we recommend trying both before committing. Measure around your natural waist (not where your trousers sit) and your lower ribs if you're considering a waist model.

Waist vs. Seat: Waist harnesses suit fast-moving, technical sailing—wave, freestyle, and racing. Seat harnesses work better for long cruises, light wind, and learning, because they distribute weight more broadly. Duotone makes genuinely good versions of both; neither is superior, only contextual.

Durability and care: Duotone windsurf harnesses are built to last 3–5 seasons with regular use. Rinse in fresh water after every session, dry fully before storing, and avoid leaving them in direct sunlight. A harness pad replacement costs far less than a new harness, so invest in that early.

FAQ

Q: Are Duotone windsurf harnesses suitable for beginners?
A: Yes. The Duotone Explorer seat harness is specifically designed for learning—it provides stability and comfort without overcomplicating things. Beginners benefit from the clear pressure distribution; you can focus on technique rather than fighting your harness.

Q: What's the difference between Duotone's Sentient and Phenom waist harnesses?
A: The Sentient prioritises weight and packability for dynamic, technical sailing. The Phenom adds extra lumbar support and is better for long sessions or heavier sailors. Both use the same buckle system; it's mainly about core support and padding density.

Q: Can I use a Duotone windsurf harness for kiteboarding?
A: No. Windsurf and kiteboard harnesses are designed differently. Kite harnesses need a spreader bar positioned much lower to manage kite forces differently. Always use the right harness for your discipline.

If you need help choosing between chest zips, back zips, or sleeveless vests, our staff will guide you through the details. We also honour returns and exchanges without hassle, because fit matters.

Our Duotone Windsurf Wetsuits Range

Winter Suits (5/4 mm and 6/5 mm): Built for cold Atlantic and North Sea conditions, these feature Duotone's signature thermal linings and reinforced seams. Models like the Duotone Winter Pro series offer exceptional flexibility in extreme cold without the bulk of traditional heavy wetsuits.

Spring/Autumn Suits (4/3 mm): Our best-sellers. Versatile across most European seasons, these Duotone windsurfing wetsuits balance warmth and mobility. The mid-season cuts work well for transitional water temperatures and are kind to your shoulders during long sessions.

Summer & Warm-Water Vests (2/2 mm and Sleeveless): Lightweight chest-zip and back-zip options ideal for Mediterranean conditions or protected bays. These Duotone wetsuits keep you legal and comfortable without overheating.

Specialty Models: Duotone also offers hooded suits for extreme cold, plus dedicated knee and elbow pads if you're pushing hard in choppy water.

Expert Advice

Duotone windsurfing wetsuits run true to size, but torso length matters more than you'd think. Measure from your neck to the top of your hip; if you're between sizes, go longer rather than shorter—you can always trim a neck seal if needed. Wrist and ankle seals should be snug but not cutting off circulation.

In colder months, pair a 5/4 mm Duotone suit with hood and booties. For spring and autumn sailing, a 4/3 mm gives you the sweet spot between warmth and range of motion. Summer sailors in the Med often prefer sleeveless vests over full suits for breathability on land.

Check seams carefully on arrival—Duotone uses glued and stitched construction, which means you're getting a suit built to last, not disposable gear. If you're new to windsurfing or upgrading from budget options, these wetsuits will feel like a revelation in durability and comfort.

FAQ

Q: How long do Duotone windsurfing wetsuits typically last?
A: With proper care (rinse in fresh water, dry flat, store out of direct sun), expect 3–5 seasons of regular use. The glued-and-stitched construction resists delamination longer than cheaper alternatives.

Q: Can I wear a Duotone 4/3 mm suit in winter, or do I need 5/4 mm?
A: It depends on your cold tolerance and water temperature. Below 12°C, most sailors prefer 5/4 mm or thicker. A 4/3 mm works if you layer with a thermal rashguard or if you're in sheltered, slightly warmer water. Test in a shop fitting room if possible.

Q: Are Duotone windsurfing wetsuits machine-washable?
A: No. Always hand-wash in cool fresh water with mild soap, then hang to dry flat. Machine washing and hot water will degrade the neoprene and seals. This care routine keeps your suit in peak condition.

Expert Advice

Blade Size: Smaller blades (170–180 sq in) suit lighter paddlers and technical poling; larger blades (190–210+ sq in) suit heavier paddlers and open-water touring. Don't assume bigger is better—it depends on your weight, fitness, and environment.

Shaft Material: Duotone's carbon shafts are noticeably lighter and provide better feedback than aluminium, but come at higher cost. Fiberglass options offer durability for beginners and casual use.

Blade Flex: Stiffer blades (carbon) feel more responsive but demand good technique. Softer blades are more forgiving and reduce fatigue over long sessions.

Length: A general rule is paddle length = board height + 6–8 inches, but this varies by blade design and paddler preference. Test different lengths if you can; even 2 inches makes a noticeable difference.

Visit surf-store.com to browse our current Duotone SUP paddles stock, read reviews from other paddlers, and get in touch with our team if you need personalised guidance.

FAQ

Q: What size Duotone SUP paddle do I need?
A: Start with your board height plus 6–8 inches. A 6'2" paddler on a 9-foot board typically uses a 230–240 cm paddle. Lighter paddlers and smaller blades may go shorter; heavier paddlers and larger blades may go longer. We recommend testing if possible, as personal preference varies.

Q: Are Duotone SUP paddles suitable for beginners?
A: Yes. Duotone's Allround Series is designed with beginners in mind—durable, forgiving, and comfortable. Their Performance Series suits intermediate and advanced paddlers seeking lighter weight and faster response.

Q: How do I care for my Duotone SUP paddle?
A: Rinse with fresh water after saltwater use. Dry thoroughly before storage. Avoid leaving it in direct sun for extended periods, as UV exposure can degrade materials. Check the shaft and blade regularly for cracks or delamination. Most Duotone paddles last 5–10 years with proper care.

Cabrinha's current lineup includes their premium Cabrinha performance line, engineered for tournament-level riders and serious wave sessions, as well as their accessible entry-level options ideal for beginners finding their feet in cold water. Here's what you'll find:

  • Winter Suits (5/4 mm and 6/5 mm): Full-length chest-zip and back-zip designs with sealed seams and reinforced knee panels. Perfect for Northern European winters and Atlantic autumn.
  • Spring/Autumn Steamers (3/2 mm and 4/3 mm): Lightweight, flexible options that strike the balance between warmth and mobility during shoulder seasons.
  • Summer Springs (2/1 mm): Minimal insulation for Mediterranean sessions or warm-water trips where you need sun protection and a bit of thermal help.
  • Kids' Suits: Same design philosophy applied to younger riders, with extra reinforcement in high-wear areas.

Expert Advice

When choosing Cabrinha wetsuits, thickness isn't the only factor. Consider your local water temperature and how long you typically session. A 5/4 mm winter suit rated for 8–12 °C water will feel restrictive in 14 °C autumn conditions; a 3/2 mm steamer is often the smarter year-round choice if you only session 2–3 times weekly. Pay attention to chest-zip versus back-zip: chest-zip suits are easier to put on and shed heat quickly if you warm up, while back-zip designs offer better seal integrity for extended sessions in cold water.

Size-wise, Cabrinha wetsuits run true to chart, but we recommend trying on before committing if you're new to the brand. Fit should feel snug without restricting breathing or shoulder mobility. If you're between sizes, opt for the smaller: neoprene stretches after a few sessions, and a baggy suit flushes water constantly, defeating the purpose of insulation.

Finally, rinse your Cabrinha suit in fresh water after every session, hang it flat in the shade to dry, and store it away from direct sunlight. This simple routine will keep your suit supple and functional for 3–5 seasons—far longer than suits treated carelessly.

Q: What thickness of Cabrinha wetsuits do I need for autumn kitesurfing in the Atlantic?

Q: Are Cabrinha wetsuits suitable for windsurfing as well as kitesurfing?

  • Light-wind variants use thinner canopy materials and refined profiles to generate power in marginal breezes—crucial during European summer lulls.

Expert Advice

Size selection matters more with wings than any other foil sport discipline. Don't assume your kiteboard size translates directly. Second: test before you commit to a full quiver. We're happy to discuss hiring or loaner programmes so you can validate your size and style choice. Cabrinha wing foiling wings are an investment; getting it right the first time saves money and frustration.

Third: inspect your wing regularly, especially if you're flying in gusty, choppy conditions common to European inland lakes and coastal zones. Check battens for flex consistency, leading-edge integrity, and line attachment points. A small repair now prevents a season-ending failure later.

Wave & Freestyle Specialist Masts – Shorter lengths (370–390 cm) paired with lighter carbon schedules. These Gaastra windsurf masts give you snap in the take-off, quick response to pressure changes, and the forgiveness you need when landing manoeuvres.

Expert Advice

Match Mast Stiffness to Your Sails & Wind Window – Gaastra publishes flex ratings (soft, medium, stiff) for a reason. We recommend starting with the flex rating your sail designer specifies, then adjusting based on your local wind conditions.

Carbon vs Aluminium – Why We've Moved On – Gaastra windsurf masts in carbon are lighter, dampen vibration better, and hold their flex curve longer. Yes, they cost more upfront, but the reduced arm fatigue and improved sail control justify the investment, especially if you're on the water 3+ days a week.

Check Your Boom Compatibility – Gaastra masts use standard IMCS diameter (29 mm and 31 mm). Before ordering, verify your boom's diameter and that your mast comes with the correct extension (most Gaastra windsurf masts arrive with adjustable extensions). Our team can help you confirm fitment.

Maintenance Extends Lifespan – Rinse with fresh water after each session, especially in salt water. Store upright in a cool, dry space. Carbon masts can last 5–10 years with care; we've seen first-hand how riders abuse gear and extend it far longer.

Q: What's the difference between Gaastra windsurf masts and competitors like NeilPryde or Duotone?

Layer consideration. Wearing a rash guard or thin thermal underneath improves comfort and helps the vest stay positioned. Avoid thick layers that create gaps.

Our ION Kite Bags Range

  • Single & Dual Kite Bags: Designed for riders carrying one or two kites, these bags feature padded compartments and durable ripstop nylon. Models like the ION Kite Bag protect your canopy from impacts and UV damage during storage and transport.
  • Travel Kite Bags: For multi-week trips or when you're bringing your entire quiver, ION's larger kite bags offer cavernous storage with reinforced handles and wheeled options. These bags are built to withstand airport baggage handlers.
  • Integrated Gear Bags: ION also manufactures gear bags that combine kite storage with dedicated compartments for bars, lines, pump, and safety gear. Models include ventilation panels to dry damp equipment and organisational pockets for accessories.
  • Boom Bags & Bar Cases: Specialised protection for bars, booms, and control systems ensures your entire kit arrives in one piece.

Expert Advice: Choosing Your ION Kite Bag

Selecting the right kite bag depends on three factors. First, consider your quiver size: one kite? Single bag. Two or three? Look at dual or travel-sized models. Second, assess your travel frequency—local riders need different protection than those flying to Morocco monthly. Third, think about organisation: do you want everything in one space, or prefer dedicated compartments?

We recommend ION kite bags with ventilation panels if you frequently pack damp equipment; the airflow prevents mildew and extends the lifespan of your canopy and lines. For frequent flyers, wheeled models reduce strain on your shoulders. And always choose bags with reinforced bottom panels—they take the most punishment.

Pro tip: most ION gear bags accommodate a range of kite sizes, but it's worth confirming with us. Overstuffing—even slightly—puts stress on zips and seams. Better to have a bag with breathing room.

Q: Can ION kite bags fit different kite brands (Duotone, Cabrinha, NeilPryde)?

Q: Are ION kite bags waterproof?

Q: How long do ION kite bags typically last?
A: With proper care, ION kite bags last 5–10 years or more. The brand's reputation rests on durability. We've seen riders get a decade from a single bag with minimal wear. Occasional zip replacements or seam repairs are far cheaper than buying new.

About Fanatic Windsurf Foils

Fanatic has been engineering high-performance windsurfing foils for over two decades, building a reputation for precision manufacturing and real-world durability. This adaptability is why serious riders choose Fanatic.

Why Buy Fanatic Windsurf Foils at Surf Store?

Our team has logged hundreds of hours on Fanatic equipment across the Adriatic, Atlantic, and Baltic—we can tell you exactly how each foil behaves in light wind, choppy swell, and spring gales. That experience matters when you're choosing between models.

Beyond stock depth, we offer detailed after-sales support: rig setup advice, maintenance guidance, and honest feedback on whether a particular foil suits your progression. We ship across Europe, often within 2–3 working days. And if you're unsure whether you need a Fanatic foil or a competitor's design, we'll talk you through the trade-offs without bias.

Our Fanatic Windsurf Foils Range

Wave Foils
Designed for immediate response and control in choppy conditions, our wave-specific Fanatic windsurf foils feature shorter fuselage lengths and refined profiles. Models like the Fanatic Wave Foil excel in European beach breaks, delivering the precision you need for tight rail-to-rail turns.

Freeride Foils
If you want versatility—gliding in light wind, holding edge in moderate conditions, and staying locked in at 20+ knots—freeride foils are your answer. Fanatic's freeride offerings provide forgiving handling and smooth delivery across a wide wind range.

Freestyle Foils
Freestyle-focused Fanatic windsurf foils prioritise spin-friendliness and rapid transitions. These models support committed tricks without sacrificing glide, ideal for park sessions or flat-water practice.

Fuselage and Wing Options
Fanatic supplies foils in modular configurations—you can upgrade your mast or wing independently as technology evolves or as your skills demand. This modularity saves money and keeps your setup current without replacing everything at once.

Expert Advice

Choosing Your Fanatic Foil
Start by defining your primary conditions: do you sail mostly waves, open water, or a mix? Your local wind profile and water state matter more than the brand name. A wave-specific Fanatic foil will underwhelm in flat-water freestyle, so honesty about your sessions is crucial.

Sizing and Mast Compatibility
Fanatic foils are tuned for specific mast lengths—typically 75–85 cm for modern designs. Confirm your board's mast-track range before ordering. If you're upgrading from an older setup, we can advise on any transition period.

Maintenance
Rinse your foil in fresh water after every session, especially salt-water use. Store it upright or horizontal in a padded bag. Fanatic foils are robust, but impact with rocks or the seabed will crack the profile or fuselage. Inspect leading edges regularly for micro-fractures.

FAQ

Q: Are Fanatic windsurf foils compatible with other brands' boards?
A: Most modern boards use standard M8 or AM80 mast tracks, so yes, compatibility is generally straightforward. We recommend checking your board's specifications and confirming the footprint before purchasing.

Q: What's the difference between Fanatic's wave and freeride foil profiles?
A: Wave foils are shorter and more responsive to small inputs; freeride foils carry more glide and stability across variable wind. Your sailing style—committed manoeuvres vs. cruising and progression—should guide the choice.

Q: Do Fanatic windsurf foils come with a warranty?
A: Yes, Fanatic offers a 12-month manufacturer's defect warranty. We handle claims directly and maintain spare parts stock for quick repairs or replacements if needed.

Our Fanatic Foil SUP Boards Range

Fanatic's current foil SUP lineup includes several purpose-built models. The Fanatic SUP foil board range spans performance-oriented designs for experienced riders as well as accessible options for those new to foiling.

  • All-Rounder Models: Balanced designs offering stability and glide for flat-water cruising and light swell. These boards forgive paddling mistakes and make foiling feel natural from day one.
  • Performance Carvers: Refined shapes for riders who've found their rhythm and want snappier edge response and quicker transitions. Built with premium carbon construction for responsiveness without sacrificing durability.
  • Wave-Riding Foil Boards: Shorter, more manoeuvrable shapes designed to handle wind-driven swell and coastal conditions. Perfect for wing foiling crossovers or traditional wave foiling in heavier water.

Each model comes in multiple sizes, from compact 75-litre options for heavier riders to nimble 60-litre designs for lighter paddlers or advanced foilers. We stock the most popular configurations and can source others quickly.

Expert Advice

Choosing the right Fanatic SUP foil board depends on your experience level, body weight, local conditions, and budget. Here's our guidance:

  • New to Foiling? Start with a volume-appropriate all-rounder. You want enough flotation to make pop-ups easy and to stay upright during learning phases. Most beginners thrive on boards in the 70–90-litre range.
  • Upgrading from Solid Boards? If you already ride non-foil SUP boards, drop the volume by roughly 30%. Foil boards generate lift, so you need less buoyancy underfoot.

Expert Advice

When choosing a Point7 windsurf mast, start with size. Your mast length should match your sail's IMCS rating—this is non-negotiable for performance and durability. Most sailors benefit from owning two Point7 windsurf masts: one around 430 cm for medium winds and freestyle, and a shorter 370–400 cm wave mast for heavy air and radical conditions.

Consider the flex curve. Point7 offers different flex profiles to suit different sailing styles. Wave sailors often prefer stiffer tops; light-wind cruisers benefit from softer, more forgiving masts. Our Kite Foils Range

Our kite foils and foil gear selection spans entry-level stability to advanced freestyle and light-wind performance:

Duotone Foil Systems: Known for refined wing shapes and durable carbon construction. The Duotone range includes ultra-stable freeride wings for learners and responsive freestyle wings for tricks.

Cabrinha Foils: Built for pure performance. Cabrinha's kite foils kitesurfing collection features high-aspect wings and lightweight masts, ideal if you're pushing speed and height.

NeilPryde Foil Wings & Masts: Engineered in Hawaii, tested globally. NeilPryde offers a breadth of sizes and stiffnesses, from beginner-friendly stability to advanced wave and freestyle rigs.

Fanatic & JP Australia Foils: Premium options for technical riders. Both brands deliver exceptional build quality and performance in their specialist categories.

Expert Advice: Buying the Right Kite Foils

Wing Size: Lighter riders and light-wind zones favour larger wings (2000–2500 cm²). Heavier riders or strong-wind spots suit smaller, stiffer wings. Beginners benefit from volume-rich, stable designs.

Mast Length & Stiffness: Longer masts (85–100 cm) suit larger wings and provide clearance in choppy water. Shorter masts (70–80 cm) suit freestyle. Stiffer masts demand better technique but reward precision.

Fuselage Type: Box fuselages are durable and accessible. Aluminium fuselages offer lightweight responsiveness. Carbon variants balance weight and durability for advanced riders.

Board Match: Ensure your kite foils setup pairs with a hydrofoil-compatible board. Most modern kitesurfing boards accept standard mast boxes; check your gear before ordering foil components.

Visit surf-store.com to browse our complete kite foils kitesurfing range, compare models, and contact our team with any questions. We're here to help you find the perfect foil setup.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What size kite foil wing do I need?
A: It depends on your weight, wind conditions, and skill level. Lighter riders in light wind typically use 2200–2500 cm² wings; heavier riders or strong-wind spots favour 1400–1800 cm² wings. Our team can recommend the ideal size—contact us before ordering.

Q: Can I use different wing and mast brands together?
A: Yes, most modern kite foils use standardised fittings. However, wing loading and flex characteristics must match for safety and performance. We recommend pairing components from the same brand or consulting our staff.

Q: How do I maintain and store my kite foils?
A: Rinse with fresh water after every session, dry thoroughly, and store in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight. Carbon components are durable but benefit from gentle handling. For detailed care guides, check our blog or ask our team when you order your foil gear.

Each type delivers different benefits, and choosing the right one depends on your riding style, body shape, and local wind patterns.

Our team has logged hundreds of hours in every harness we sell—we know which models suit narrow frames, which offer the best lumbar support, and which keep their shape after a season of hard use. We ship fast, handle returns fairly, and provide honest advice if you're between two models—because the wrong choice wastes money and ruins progression.

Our Kitesurfing Harnesses Range

Waist Harnesses: These sit low on your hips, leaving your torso mobile. The Duotone Link Waist and Cabrinha Apex Waist are benchmarks—lightweight, secure boom-cup integration, and minimal bulk under your jacket. Ideal if you ride tricks, race, or prefer a stripped-down feel.

Seat Harnesses: Built for comfort and power in 15+ knot conditions. The ION Apex Seat features a curved lumbar insert and thigh padding that stays soft through salt water and sun. The Nobile Foundation Seat offers exceptional durability and wind range; many of our customers ride it for two or three seasons without wear. Seat models excel in UK and Atlantic spots where wind is reliable but intense.

Hybrid Options: Brands are blurring the line—some newer waist designs add subtle thigh support, while compact seat models reduce bulk. Check our current 2026 stock for the latest innovations.

Expert Advice

Fit is everything. A loose harness rolls under load, kills control, and causes bruising. A too-tight harness cuts circulation. When you order from us, measure your natural waist and hip circumference. We'll recommend sizing and will arrange a return if the fit isn't right first time—no hassle.

Match your weight range and wind. Heavier riders and strong-wind spots demand a seat model with robust stitching and wide spreader bars. Lighter riders, light winds, and trick focus suit waist harnesses. If you're split between two, ask us—we've been there.

Care extends life. Rinse in fresh water after each session, dry fully before storage, and inspect boom-cup stitching before heavy use. A damaged harness is unsafe; we stock replacement loops and can advise on repair.

Layering matters. If you wear a thick jacket in winter, size up slightly so the harness doesn't compress padding. In summer rashguards, you can go true-to-size.

FAQ

Q: Should I choose a waist or seat kitesurfing harness?
A: Waist harnesses suit lighter winds, freestyle tricks, and riders who prioritise mobility. Seat harnesses are best for strong wind, long sessions, and maximum comfort. Most serious kiteboarders own both.

Q: How do I measure for the correct kitesurfing harness size?
A: Measure your natural waist (not over clothing) and your hip circumference at the widest point. Cross-reference both with the brand's size chart—sizing varies. If you're between sizes, contact us and we'll recommend which way to go based on fit preference.

Q: How long does a kitesurfing harness last?
A: With care, a quality harness lasts 2–4 seasons. Regular freshwater rinsing, storage away from direct sun, and inspection of stitching extends life. Look for soft padding breakdown and boom-cup wear as signs of aging.

Choose the Right Harness: Your harness is the interface between you and your kite. FAQ

Q: What's the difference between waist and seat harnesses?

Q: How often should I replace my control lines?

Q: Do I need impact protection if I'm not learning tricks?

Our Impact Vests Range

Duotone Impact Protection combines minimalist design with maximum safety. Their vests are favoured by wave riders and freestyle athletes who want lightweight protection that doesn't restrict movement. Duotone's engineering means you feel the water and wind clearly while staying protected.

Cabrinha Safety Vests appeal to all-round riders seeking versatile, affordable protection. These vests balance cost and performance, making them ideal for beginners and intermediate riders building their quiver.

All our kitesurfing impact vests safety options come in XS to XXL sizing and feature adjustable straps to dial in the perfect fit. Whether you're learning tricks, riding waves, or pushing your limits in stronger conditions, we have the right vest in stock.

Expert Advice

Fit is Everything: An impact vest that's too loose won't protect you; one that's too tight restricts breathing and movement. Measure your chest and rib cage accurately. Most riders size down in neoprene vests compared to regular clothing.

Padding Placement: Check where the foam sits. For freestyle tricks, you want maximum rib and sternum padding. For wave riding, some protection on the shoulders and back helps too.

Material Matters: Premium neoprene stays flexible in cold water and lasts longer. Look for vests with sealed seams to prevent water leakage and chafing.

Seasonality: In summer, thinner 2mm vests work well. For European winter sessions, consider 3–4mm neoprene for warmth plus protection.

Visit surf-store.com to browse our full range, read detailed specifications, and find the perfect kitesurfing impact vests safety vest for your style and conditions.

FAQ

Q: Can I use a regular buoyancy aid instead of an impact vest?

Q: How often should I replace my impact vest?

Q: Do impact vests work in cold water?
A: Yes. Neoprene vests provide insulation plus protection. In winter, a 3–4mm vest keeps you warmer while still absorbing impacts. For extreme cold, layer under a winter jacket.

These aren't generic sports bags—they're engineered specifically for the shape and fragility of modern kites, harnesses, and boards.

Why Buy Kite Bags at Surf Store?

We're based in Slovenia, in the heart of Europe, and we ship kite bags and carry bags across the continent within days. Our team has real on-water experience; we know how a kite bag performs after a season of use, which zips fail first, and which designs actually keep your kit dry during a wet commute. When you order from us, you're buying from instructors and enthusiasts, not generic retailers.

We also offer expert after-sales support. If your kite bag splits or a zip fails, we'll advise you on repair or replacement options. Our pricing is transparent—we list everything in EUR—and we stock enough depth in the best models that you won't wait weeks for restock.

Our Kite Bags Range

Our kite bags and carry bags collection covers three main use cases:

  • Single-Kite Bags: Compact, lightweight carry bags designed for one kite. Ideal for day trips or travellers who pack light. Popular choices include the Duotone Kite Bag and Cabrinha Kite Bag, both featuring padded interiors and grab handles.
  • Double and Triple Kite Bags: For riders with quiver depth, these larger carry bags accommodate 2–3 kites plus harnesses and bar. The ION Kite Bag Pro is a favourite here, with dividers and ventilation slots to prevent mildew.
  • Travel & Board Bags: Full-size kite bags that also house your board and foil. The NeilPryde Travel Bag is tough enough for courier services and airline hold baggage.

Every kite bag in our range is chosen for durability, weather resistance, and practical design. We stock colours and sizes from the latest 2026 collections, so you can match your kit or go stealth black.

Expert Advice

Size: Measure your largest kite (e.g., 17m) before buying. A kite bag that's too snug will strain zips and seams; too loose and your kite will shift during transport.

Material: Look for UV-stabilised nylon or canvas outer shells. Padded interiors protect against punctures. If you live near salt water, prioritise stainless-steel zips—they resist corrosion far better than standard brass.

Ventilation: Kites stored damp develop mildew and smell terrible. Choose a kite bag with mesh panels or perforated ventilation; some modern designs even include silica pockets to absorb moisture.

Portability: Grab handles, shoulder straps, and wheel compatibility matter if you're catching trains or buses. Our ION and Cabrinha ranges excel here; they nest atop standard luggage.

If you're unsure which carry bag suits your needs, email us or call. We'll match you to the right model based on your quiver size and how often you travel.

FAQ

Q: Do I need a separate kite bag for each kite, or can one large carry bag fit all of them?
A: One large kite bag is usually more practical and space-efficient than multiple single bags, especially if you travel. The ION Kite Bag Pro holds 2–3 kites comfortably. If you store kites at home long-term, individual kite bags offer better ventilation and take up less shelf space.

Q: Can a kite bag protect my equipment during air travel?
A: Yes, but invest in a travel-grade carry bag with reinforced corners and stainless-steel zips. The NeilPryde Travel Bag is airline-approved and has survived dozens of trans-European flights in our testers' hands. Avoid soft single-kite bags for checked luggage; choose a hard-sided option or a heavily padded travel model.

Q: How do I stop my kite bag from smelling of mildew?
A: Always dry your kite thoroughly before packing it away. Store your carry bag in a cool, dry place with the zips open. Bags with ventilation slots or mesh panels help moisture escape. Some riders add silica gel packets inside; change them monthly in humid climates.

description_html:
About Windsurf Boards

Windsurf boards are the heart of modern windsurfing—they've evolved from solid, heavy hulls into refined shapes engineered for speed, control, and progression. At Surf Store, we stock the world's leading windsurfing manufacturers: JP Australia, Duotone, Fanatic, Gaastra, and Tabou. Whether you're chasing performance in freeride or building foundational skills as a beginner, modern windsurf boards deliver exceptional value. They're lighter, stronger, and more forgiving than ever—allowing you to progress faster and enjoy every session on the water.

Why Buy Windsurf Boards at Surf Store?

We're not just a retailer—we're water sports instructors and serious enthusiasts. Our team has real on-water experience with every windsurf board we stock. We understand the difference between a board that looks good on spec and one that feels right in your hands.

  • Expert stock curation: We carry proven models from JP Australia, Duotone, Fanatic, and Tabou—no filler, only boards that deliver results.
  • Personalised guidance: We'll help you choose the right size, volume, and type based on your weight, skill level, wind conditions, and riding style.
  • Fast EU shipping: Orders ship quickly across Europe. Prices shown in EUR with no surprises at checkout.
  • After-sales support: Questions about setup, maintenance, or technique? We're here to help.

Our Windsurf Boards Range

Beginner Windsurf Boards
Starting out? Look to boards with higher volume, wider platforms, and forgiving flex characteristics. These windsurf boards excel at flotation and stability, letting you focus on technique without fighting the equipment. JP Australia's entry-level models and Fanatic's beginner range deliver excellent progression without compromise.

Freeride Windsurf Boards
The sweet spot for most riders—freeride windsurf boards balance speed, manoeuvrability, and ease of use. Models like the Duotone Spike and Tabou Pocket offer responsive handling, good glide, and confidence in variable conditions. These boards reward technique while remaining accessible to intermediates pushing their limits.

Wave & Freestyle Boards
Specialised designs for specific disciplines. Wave boards are narrower, shorter, and highly manoeuvrable. Freestyle boards feature reinforced decks and pop for tricks. Both demand precision but unlock new riding possibilities once you've mastered the basics on freeride windsurf boards.

Performance & Slalom Boards
For experienced riders chasing speed and efficiency. These windsurf boards feature sharp rails, refined foil, and premium materials (carbon constructions) to maximise glide and acceleration.

Expert Advice: Choosing the Right Windsurf Boards

Size matters most. Volume should match your body weight plus sail weight. Beginners typically need 15–20% more volume than their body weight in litres; experienced riders can go lighter. Check the spec sheet carefully.

Width and length. Wider boards (70+ cm) offer easier entry and more flotation; narrower boards (62–68 cm) are faster but demand better technique. Length varies by volume and brand philosophy—longer boards generally glide better, shorter boards turn faster.

Construction & materials. Carbon-reinforced windsurf boards are lighter and more responsive but cost more. Wooden cores offer durability and warmth in cold water. Understand what matters for your conditions and budget.

Flex and feel. Some boards are stiffer (more direct feedback, higher performance ceiling); others flex more (more forgiving, smoother ride). Neither is objectively better—it's about preference and progression stage.

Visit surf-store.com to browse our windsurf boards stock, filter by size and type, and reach out with questions. We're here to help you find the perfect board.

FAQ

Q: What size windsurf board should I choose?
A: Match the board's volume to your body weight. Beginners should aim for volume roughly equal to their body weight in kilos (for example, 75 kg rider = 75 litres minimum). Intermediate and experienced riders can use 5–10 litres less. Always check manufacturer guidelines and consider your sail size and local wind range.

Q: What's the difference between freeride and beginner windsurf boards?
A: Beginner windsurf boards prioritise stability, flotation, and forgiveness—wider, higher volume, and more flexible. Freeride boards are narrower, lighter, and more responsive, demanding better technique but rewarding it with speed and manoeuvrability. Start on beginner boards; progress to freeride as your skills improve.

Q: Which brands of windsurf boards does Surf Store stock?
A: We carry JP Australia, Duotone, Fanatic, Gaastra, and Tabou—all proven brands with strong heritage and innovative designs. Each offers models across beginner, freeride, wave, and performance categories. Check our website for current models and availability.

Why Buy SUP Boards at Surf Store?

We've been riding and recommending water sports equipment for years, and that experience informs every stand up paddle board we stock. Our team has paddled the boards we sell. Our SUP Boards Range

All-Round and Touring Boards
Our bestselling category. Models like the Fanatic Viper and JP Australia Tour series are built for distance paddling, light chop tolerance, and forgiving learning curves. These stand up paddle boards prioritise glide and stability, making them ideal for flat-water exploration and fitness paddling. Widths typically range from 28" to 32", with lengths from 10' to 12'6".

Race and Performance Boards
For riders chasing speed: Tabou and JP Australia offer race-focused SUP boards with narrow profiles, pronounced rocker, and premium construction. These boards demand better technique but reward you with efficiency and pace. Designed for paddlers with existing experience.

Wave SUP Boards
Fanatic's Wave series brings SUP into the surf zone. Shorter, more manoeuvrable, with reinforced construction for impact and recovery. If you want to ride white water or small waves on a paddle board, these dedicated models will transform your session.

Inflatable SUP Boards
Portability and durability in one package. Our inflatable stand up paddle boards use military-grade PVC and drop-stitch technology to match the rigidity of hardboards whilst remaining packable and storage-friendly. Perfect for holiday trips and regular paddlers without garage space.

Expert Advice

Sizing matters. Width affects stability; length affects glide and tracking. Heavier or newer paddlers typically benefit from boards 30"+ wide and 10' or longer. Lighter, experienced riders can go narrower and shorter without sacrificing control.

Intended use is your north star. Are you paddling flat water? Tour boards are your friend. Keen to paddle into waves? Wave SUP boards are purpose-built. Racing twice a week? A race board's performance premium justifies the learning curve.

Construction quality matters for longevity. Our boards use epoxy or polyester resin, quality foam cores, and reinforced decks. A well-maintained SUP board should last years of regular use. We stock boards priced from entry-level through premium, and better builds last longer—factor that into your decision.

Paddle and leash come separately. Budget for a quality paddle matched to your height and a secure ankle leash. These aren't luxuries; they're safety and performance essentials.

FAQ

Q: What size stand up paddle board should a beginner buy?
A: Beginners benefit from boards 10' to 11' long and 30" to 32" wide—maximum stability and forgiveness. Weight capacity matters: choose a board rated 20–30 kg above your body weight. Models like the Fanatic Viper or JP Australia Tour are ideal entry points.

Q: Are inflatable SUP boards as rigid as hardboards?
A: Modern inflatable stand up paddle boards using drop-stitch technology rival hardboards for rigidity and feel on the water. The trade-off is durability—they're more puncture-resistant for travel but less impact-tough than foam-core boards used in rocky conditions.

Q: How do I choose between touring and race SUP boards?
A: Tour boards prioritise comfort, stability, and glide across varied conditions. Race boards sacrifice width and comfort for speed and efficiency—they demand better paddling technique. Choose touring unless you're training for speed or racing events regularly.

Stand-up paddleboarding has evolved from a niche activity into one of Europe's most accessible water sports, and the quality of your SUP accessories makes all the difference between a frustrating session and a genuinely enjoyable day on the water. The best SUP accessories paddles leashes and safety gear reflect decades of refinement—lighter materials, smarter designs, and proven durability born from real testing in challenging European conditions.

Why Buy SUP Accessories at Surf Store?

We're not just another online retailer. Our team has logged thousands of hours on SUP boards across Slovenian lakes, Mediterranean coasts, and Atlantic swells. When you browse our SUP accessories selection, you're looking at products we've personally tested and refined recommendations for. Our paddles have been shaped in different wind conditions; our leashes have been stress-tested in currents; our safety harnesses have been worn during long sessions.

We ship rapidly across the EU, stock depth is genuine (not dropshipped), and if something doesn't work, our after-sales team will help you find the right alternative. We also provide detailed sizing guides and video comparisons so you make confident choices.

Our SUP Accessories Range

Paddles
Your paddle is your primary control tool. We stock adjustable-length aluminium paddles for beginners, lightweight carbon options for distance and touring, and reinforced designs for flatwater SUP racing. Point7 and Duotone paddles dominate our range because of their balanced weight-to-strength ratio and ergonomic blade design. Adjustability matters—we carry paddles from 150–230 cm to suit riders from 150 cm to 200+ cm tall.

Leashes
A quality leash keeps your board from drifting away and tethers you safely to your equipment. Our SUP accessories include coiled and straight leashes in 2.4 m, 3 m, and longer formats depending on your board size and water conditions. ION and Cabrinha leashes feature reinforced ankle cuffs and UV-resistant cord; they're built for European winter conditions and rough handling.

Wetsuits and Water Apparel
SUP sessions often extend longer than you expect. ION wetsuits designed for flatwater and light paddling offer thermal protection without bulk, keeping your shoulders and torso warm without restricting paddle stroke. Matching rash guards and board shorts round out safe, comfortable session wear.

Safety and Navigation
Leashes are only the start. We stock PFDs (personal flotation devices), dry bags, GPS mounts, and navigation accessories. If you're paddling beyond sight of shore, proper safety gear isn't negotiable.

Board Care and Repair
Dings happen. We stock repair kits, board bags, and protective covers so your SUP investment lasts seasons, not months.

Expert Advice

Choosing the Right Paddle
Paddle weight matters more than you think. A heavy paddle fatigues your shoulders; a too-light paddle feels flimsy in chop. We recommend carbon-reinforced blades for anyone paddling more than twice weekly. Length depends on your board width and your height; an oversized paddle will force poor technique. Visit us or contact our team for a personal recommendation.

Leash Selection
Match your leash diameter to your water temperature and risk tolerance. A thicker, stiffer leash offers more security in currents; a flexible leash absorbs shock better in choppy conditions. For year-round European paddling, we suggest keeping two leashes—a summer option and a winter one for cold-water sessions.

Accessory Bundles
New to SUP? Start with a paddle, leash, and entry-level PFD. Our staff can suggest complete beginner setups that keep costs reasonable while ensuring safety and comfort.

FAQ

Q: What length leash do I need for my SUP board?
A: Choose a leash length equal to or slightly longer than your board length. Most recreational boards use 2.4–3 m leashes. Longer leashes (4–5 m) suit touring boards and downwind paddling; shorter leashes (2 m) work for crowded areas or racing. Check your board's leash attachment point and your own paddling style.

Q: Can I use a windsurfing or kitesurfing harness for SUP?
A: Not recommended. SUP paddles and body mechanics differ significantly. SUP-specific harnesses (if used at all) distribute force differently. Most SUP paddlers don't wear harnesses; instead, focus on core strength and proper paddle technique. Always prioritise a personal flotation device over other gear.

Q: Do I need a wetsuit for SUP in summer?
A: A lightweight rash guard or UPF-rated shirt is wise to prevent sunburn and minor chafing. If water temperature is above 20°C and air temperature comfortable, a thin neoprene shorts or 2 mm shorty offers flexibility. Cold-water paddlers (below 15°C) should wear a 3 mm or thicker wetsuit to stay safe and comfortable for extended sessions.

description_html:
About Wing Foiling Harnesses

Wing foiling harnesses have evolved rapidly since the discipline exploded in popularity. Unlike traditional waist harnesses built for kitesurfing or windsurfing, modern wing foiling harnesses are engineered for a completely different riding position and load distribution. You're standing upright, the wing is held at chest height, and the force is concentrated through your core rather than your hips.

The leading brands—ION, Duotone, and Cabrinha—have spent years refining waist and chest harness designs specifically for wing riders. What makes today's wing foiling harnesses stand out is their focus on low-profile construction, lightweight materials, and intelligent padding placement that keeps you comfortable during long sessions without restricting shoulder movement. Many models now feature hybrid waist-to-chest geometry, allowing you to adjust your riding style and load distribution on the fly.

Heritage matters here: ION's background in wetsuits means their harnesses prioritise ergonomic padding and stretch panels. Duotone brings kiteboarding precision engineering to wing-specific designs. These aren't repurposed products; they're purpose-built for foiling.

Expert Advice

Fit is everything with wing foiling harnesses. Unlike kitesurfing where you can often size up, wing harnesses need to sit snugly at your natural waist or chest without riding up during manoeuvres. Measure around your waist or chest (depending on harness type) whilst standing relaxed, then add 2–3 cm for comfort. Don't guess.

Padding density varies widely. If you're doing three-hour sessions, invest in thicker, high-density padding (usually found in the premium ION and Duotone models). For shorter sessions or lighter bodies, thinner padding reduces bulk and weight.

Attachment point matters. Check where your wing's bridle spreader bar will sit on your chosen harness. Some wing designs prefer a more forward or rear hook position; mismatch here can ruin your session. Our staff can cross-reference your wing model with harness geometry.

Colour and visibility. Bright colours (yellow, orange) in your wing foiling harness improve visibility to other water users—essential in European watersports spots where you might share breaks with swimmers or other board riders.

FAQ

Q: Can I use a kiteboarding harness for wing foiling?
A: Not ideally. Kiteboarding harnesses are designed for sit-on boards with different load angles. Wing foiling harnesses are lower-profile and built for the standing, upright position wing riding demands. You'll sacrifice comfort and control using a kite harness.

Q: What's the difference between waist and chest wing foiling harnesses?
A: Waist harnesses distribute load through your hips and are lighter and less restrictive. Chest harnesses place load on your shoulders and torso, offering more support and power transfer—better for strong wind or heavier riders. Many riders own both and switch depending on conditions.

Q: How long do wing foiling harnesses typically last?
A: With proper care—rinsing after saltwater, storing dry, avoiding extended UV exposure—quality harnesses from ION or Duotone last 3–5 years of regular use. Replacement pads and spreader bars extend that life significantly and are inexpensive compared to a new harness.
